title: Cities
description: City guide index for Belgium's most visited destinations.

items.name: Brussels
items.shortDescription: Belgium's capital and the de facto capital of the EU — home to the Grand Place, EU institutions, and the largest Indian community in Belgium.

items.name: Antwerp
items.shortDescription: Belgium's largest port city, a global diamond trading hub with a significant Indian (especially Gujarati/Jain) business community.

items.name: Ghent
items.shortDescription: A medieval university city with canals, a lively student population and striking Gothic architecture.

items.name: Bruges
items.shortDescription: A UNESCO World Heritage old town famed for its canals, chocolate shops and medieval charm.

items.name: Leuven
items.shortDescription: Home to KU Leuven, one of Europe's oldest universities, and a large international student population.

items.name: Liège
items.shortDescription: A historic industrial city on the Meuse river with a French-speaking university community.

items.name: Namur
items.shortDescription: Capital of Wallonia, known for its citadel overlooking the Meuse and Sambre rivers.

items.name: Mechelen
items.shortDescription: A charming, walkable city midway between Brussels and Antwerp with a well-preserved historic centre.
